pr.title: Match ithammerhead & link, match some itscopebeam functions
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched the Hammer Head item source and build link, renamed its thrown callback from the generic it_3F14_Logic40_Thrown to itHammerHead_Logic40_Thrown, implemented several previously placeholder Hammer Head functions, and added partial Super Scope beam decompilation using explicit item-vars and beam attribute structs. The PR also made a small matching-oriented refactor in itfoods.c and updated symbols/configuration to reflect matching status and local sdata2 symbols.
what_changed: Changed ithammerhead.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
Implemented Hammer Head item state table it_803F6640 with animation, physics, and collision callbacks for its four state entries.

Implemented it_80299C48 as the Hammer Head spawn helper: initializes SpawnItem for It_Kind_Hammer_Head, copies position, zeroes z/velocity fields, sets parent gobj fields and flags, creates the item, applies facing direction and initial velocity scaling, then links debug ownership with db_80225DD8.
Implemented it_80299D7C with ground-to-air handling through it_802762BC, item setup via it_8026B390, and state change through Item_80268E5C.
Implemented itHammerHead_Logic40_Thrown and routed itHammerHead_Logic40_Dropped and the collision callback through the Hammer Head-specific thrown name.

Implemented Hammer Head life-timer animation callbacks itHammerhead_UnkMotion2_Anim and itHammerhead_UnkMotion3_Anim, including the low-life visual update call to it_802728C8 when xD44_lifeTimer reaches it_804D6D28->x34.

Renamed the symbol and declarations for the thrown Hammer Head logic from it_3F14_Logic40_Thrown to itHammerHead_Logic40_Thrown in symbols.txt, ithammerhead.h, it_2725.c, and the commented it_3F14.c logic table.
Renamed itHammerheadAttributes.x0 to initial_velocity, making the spawn math in it_80299C48 self-documenting.
Added itScopeBeam_ItemVars to itCommonItems.h and inserted scopebeam into the Item xDD4_itemVar union in types.h.
Added local BeamFloats and ScopeBeamAttrs structs in itsscopebeam.c for Super Scope beam special attributes, replacing raw pointer arithmetic in touched code.
Updated it_80298DEC to store the beam index in xDD4_itemVar.scopebeam.x0 instead of reusing starrodstar item vars.

Implemented it_80298ED0 for scope beam setup: assigns owner, selects item state by beam index, sets x velocity from attribute velocity and facing direction, applies uniform JObj scale, and initializes lifetime.
Updated itSscopebeam_UnkMotion9_Anim to read the beam index and lifetime/scale through scopebeam item vars and structured ScopeBeamAttrs instead of starrodstar fields and raw f32 frame_data.
Refactored itFoods_Logic18_Spawned to take HSD_GObj*, use a static getRandMax helper, reduce PAD_STACK from 12 to 8, and remove casts at it_80273318 and it_8028FC5C call sites.
Converted several sdata2 symbols at 0x804DCC60 through 0x804DCC70 from global it_804DCCxx names to local @151, @152, @177, and @179 labels.
smart_moves: Promoting ithammerhead.c to Matching only after implementing the spawn helper, state table, thrown logic, timer anim callbacks, and header prototypes kept the build configuration aligned with actual decomp status.

Replacing the generic it_3F14_Logic40_Thrown name with itHammerHead_Logic40_Thrown made the item logic table and collision/dropped callbacks reflect ownership by Hammer Head rather than a shared or unidentified module.
Naming the Hammer Head attribute field initial_velocity directly documents why spawn velocity multiplies both the input velocity and facing direction.
Adding a dedicated itScopeBeam_ItemVars union member avoided reusing starrodstar item-vars for Super Scope beam state, which reduces cross-item semantic confusion.

Using BeamFloats and ScopeBeamAttrs in itsscopebeam.c turned raw special-attribute pointer arithmetic into named velocity, scale, and lifetime fields, making matching code easier to review and extend.
The itfoods.c getRandMax helper appears to be a small matching tactic: preserving logic while reshaping stack usage and call expressions enough to match the target.
decomp_lessons: When an item source becomes matching, update configure.py immediately so the object is built as Matching and future regressions are visible.
Generic it_3F14 or address-based function names should be replaced with item-specific names once call-table ownership is clear.
Item xDD4_itemVar union members should be item-specific; avoid borrowing another item's item-var struct even when the layout happens to match.
For article special attributes, introduce narrow local structs when enough layout is known. Even partial structs like BeamFloats can eliminate error-prone byte offsets and clarify matching intent.
Renaming attribute fields such as x0 to behavior-derived names like initial_velocity is useful once code evidence shows how the field is consumed.
Small helper functions and PAD_STACK adjustments can be legitimate matching tools, as shown by itFoods_Logic18_Spawned and getRandMax.
Remember to propagate symbol renames through symbols.txt, headers, logic tables, and any documented/commented table mirrors to keep decomp metadata searchable and consistent.
naming_conventions: Use item-specific callback names in the form itHammerHead_Logic40_Thrown instead of generic module names like it_3F14_Logic40_Thrown when the function belongs to a known item.

Hammer Head function names in this slice use mixed capitalization already present in the project, including itHammerHead_Logic40_* for logic callbacks and itHammerhead_UnkMotion*_Anim/Phys/Coll for state callbacks.
Known attribute fields should be renamed from x-offset placeholders to semantic names when behavior is clear, as with itHammerheadAttributes.initial_velocity.
Item variable structs follow the pattern itScopeBeam_ItemVars and are added to Item.xDD4_itemVar under a lower-case item key, here scopebeam.
Small internal data-layout structs can use descriptive names such as BeamFloats and ScopeBeamAttrs even if defined locally in the C file.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed ithammerhead.c to Matching in configure.py after replacing placeholders with C implementations.
Used an explicit ItemStateTable initializer for it_803F6640, which likely helped match the item state table data layout and callback references.
In itfoods.c, extracted getRandMax(Item*) and adjusted PAD_STACK from 12 to 8, suggesting stack-shape control for matching.
Replaced casts and raw special-attribute reads with typed attribute variables while preserving generated code shape in touched functions.
Converted sdata2 entries from global it_804DCCxx symbols to local @number labels for constants newly associated with matched code.
Used switch cases 0 through 9 in it_80298ED0 to select Item_80268E5C states, avoiding a more compact but potentially nonmatching abstraction.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match itsscopebeam.c or move BeamFloats/ScopeBeamAttrs into shared headers.
Check whether the remaining itsscopebeam functions continue using scopebeam item vars instead of starrodstar item vars.
Search for other item callbacks still named it_3F14_Logic40_* that can be renamed to item-specific logic names.
Look for subsequent cleanup of Hammer Head capitalization consistency between itHammerHead and itHammerhead.
Find other PRs using helper extraction plus PAD_STACK adjustment as a matching tactic.
